title: Granola
image_alt: Homemade granola with dried fruit and nuts
modifications: Reduced sunflower seeds to ⅔ of original (1⅓ cups instead of 2)
Substituted pecans for almonds, increased to 1½ cups
Added hemp hearts and chia seeds
Removed walnuts; optional chopped chocolate added after cooling

items.name: chopped chocolate (optional, add once fully cooled)

steps: Preheat oven to 325°F (165°C). Line a large rimmed baking sheet with parchment paper.
Melt butter and honey together in a large saucepan over low heat. Whisk in the cinnamon.
Stir in oats, sunflower seeds, pecans, ground flax, hemp hearts, and chia seeds until everything is evenly coated.
Spread mixture in an even layer on the prepared pan. Bake for 15–20 minutes until golden, keeping an eye on it as ovens vary.

Remove from oven and immediately sprinkle pecans and dried fruit over the top. Let cool completely on the pan — it will crisp up as it cools. Once fully cooled, mix in chopped chocolate if using. Break into clusters and store in an airtight container.
